name: Plaid API
what_it_does: Financial data network connecting apps to bank accounts for transaction retrieval, balance checking, account verification, and ACH payment initiation with OAuth-based bank linking.
use_cases: Retrieving bank transaction history for personal finance agents
Account balance verification before payment initiation
Income and employment verification for lending workflows
ACH payment initiation and bank account authentication
Identity verification linked to financial accounts
not_for: International bank connectivity outside North America/Europe (limited coverage)
Real-time payment processing without ACH delays
Teams that can't meet Plaid's security and compliance requirements
best_when: An agent needs to access bank account data or initiate ACH payments with user consent in a compliant way.
avoid_when: You need real-time payment rails, international bank coverage, or can't handle the compliance requirements of financial data.

auth.notes: Client ID + Secret for API auth. Access tokens represent individual user bank connections (obtained via Link flow). Two-tier auth: your credentials + per-user access tokens.

pricing.notes: No public free tier. Sandbox environment is free. Production pricing based on product usage. Can become expensive at scale.

performance.rate_limits: 100 requests/minute default; higher with enterprise plans

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Structured error responses with error_type, error_code, and display_message fields. Error codes are well-documented.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Some endpoints support idempotency keys. Transaction data fetching is idempotent by nature.
known_agent_gotchas: User must complete Plaid Link UI flow to generate access token - can't be done purely programmatically
Transactions are asynchronous - new transactions may not appear immediately after bank sync
Access tokens never expire but item errors (ITEM_LOGIN_REQUIRED) require user re-authentication
Institution coverage varies - some smaller banks use scraped data which is less reliable
Webhook verification requires validating JWT with Plaid's public keys
